title: Income Tax Payment Modes: Navigating the New Era (April 29, 2026 Update)
content:
# Income Tax Payment Modes: Navigating the New Era (April 29, 2026 Update)

Hello, professional colleagues! Ek important update hai regarding tax payment modes. Government ne Income-tax Acts 1961 & 2025 ke तहत tax payments ke liye naye guidelines announce kiye hain, effective April 1, 2026.

As you know, the Income Tax Act, 2025 has officially replaced the Income Tax Act, 1961, starting from April 1, 2026. Iss transition ko accommodate karte hue, Income Tax Department ne tax payment mechanisms ko update kiya hai.

Here's what you need to know:

*   **Acts Covered:** The e-filing portal now seamlessly facilitates tax payments under both the old Income Tax Act, 1961 (for dues up to FY 2025-26/AY 2026-27) and the new Income Tax Act, 2025 (for Tax Year 2026-27 onwards).
*   **Payment Modes Remain Unchanged:** Good news! The permissible modes for remitting taxes have not been altered. You can still use the standard five payment modes:
    *   Net Banking
    *   Debit Card
    *   Pay at Bank Counter (OTC) via Cash, Cheque, or Demand Draft
    *   RTGS/NEFT
    *   Payment Gateway (Credit Card, Debit Card, UPI, or Net Banking)
*   **New Challans & Selection:** CBDT ne April 1, 2026 se naye tax payment challans rollout kiye hain on the Income Tax e-Filing Portal. While making payments, it is crucial to select the correct Act – "Income Tax Act 1961" for assessment years up to 26-27 and prior years, and "Income Tax Act 2025" for Tax Year 2026-27 onwards. Incorrect selection can lead to system-level validation errors.
*   **Simplified Framework:** The Income Tax Act, 2025, aims for a streamlined, simplified tax code with reduced compliance burden. The procedural framework for this has been established through the Income-tax Rules, 2026, notified by Notification No. 22/2026 dated March 20, 2026.

This update ensures that while the underlying tax law framework has evolved, the convenience of tax payment modes remains intact. Bas ab payment karte time sahi Act choose karna zaroori hai!

## Read the Official Document
*   [Download the Official Notification/Circular here](https://www.incometax.gov.in/)

*Published on: 30 April 2026*
